Follow for more 𝐈𝐧 𝐭𝐨𝐝𝐚𝐲’𝐬 𝐞𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐨𝐟 #𝟏𝟎𝐓𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬, 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐧𝐝 𝐚 𝐜𝐨𝐳𝐲 𝐟𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐝𝐚𝐲 𝐚𝐭 𝐡𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐢𝐧 𝐋𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬 @𝐲𝐚𝐳𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐝 (𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐲) 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐬𝐜𝐫𝐢𝐩𝐭𝐬, 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐩𝐚𝐫𝐤 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐝𝐨𝐠 𝐊𝐨𝐤𝐨 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐦𝐚𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚 𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐦 𝐜𝐮𝐩 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐞𝐚 (𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐟𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐬𝐜𝐨𝐨𝐩𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐬𝐮𝐠𝐚𝐫, 𝐧𝐨 𝐥𝐞𝐬𝐬)🐾💗 “𝐌𝐲 𝐟𝐚𝐯𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐭 𝐋𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐨𝐮𝐥𝐝 𝐛𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐜𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐈 𝐠𝐞𝐭 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐰𝐚𝐥𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐬𝐞𝐞𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐲𝐨𝐧𝐞’𝐬 𝐢𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐯𝐢𝐝𝐮𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲,” 𝐬𝐚𝐲𝐬 𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧. “𝐈 𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐟𝐞𝐞𝐥 𝐥𝐢𝐤𝐞 𝐈 𝐟𝐢𝐭 𝐢𝐧 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐟𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐥𝐲.” 𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧, 𝐰𝐡𝐨 𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐲𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐫𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐞𝐫 𝐄𝐥𝐥𝐞 𝐢𝐧 “𝐇𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐬𝐭𝐨𝐩𝐩𝐞𝐫” 𝐨𝐧 @𝐧𝐞𝐭𝐟𝐥𝐢𝐱𝐮𝐤 𝐚𝐥𝐬𝐨 𝐫𝐞𝐟𝐥𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐨𝐧 𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐞𝐱𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐟𝐢𝐥𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐒𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐨𝐧 𝟑, 𝐰𝐡𝐢𝐜𝐡 𝐫𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐞𝐝 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐰𝐞𝐞𝐤. “𝐁𝐞𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞 𝐭𝐨 𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐲 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐭𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐄𝐥𝐥𝐞 𝐡𝐚𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐬𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐚𝐬 𝐬𝐨 𝐠𝐨𝐨𝐝 𝐛𝐞𝐜𝐚𝐮𝐬𝐞 𝐈’𝐦 𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐲𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐦𝐲𝐬𝐞𝐥𝐟, 𝐛𝐚𝐬𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐲. 𝐈’𝐦 𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐝 𝐨𝐟 𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐄𝐥𝐥𝐞 𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐲 𝐬𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐨𝐧.

#🔨ᴿᵉᵃᵈʸ ᵗᵒ ᵇᵘᶦˡᵈ ᵗʰᵉ ᶠᵒᵘⁿᵈᵃᵗᶦᵒⁿ ᵒᶠ ʸᵒᵘʳ ᵃᶜᵗᶦⁿᵍ ᶜʳᵃᶠᵗ? ᴶᵒᶦⁿ ᵘˢ ᶦⁿ-ᵖᵉʳˢᵒⁿ ᵃᵗ ᵒᵘʳ ᴸᵒˢ ᴬⁿᵍᵉˡᵉˢ ˢᵗᵘᵈᶦᵒ ᶠᵒʳ ᵗʰᵉ ⁶-ᵂᵉᵉᵏ ᴬᶜᵗᶦⁿᵍ ᵀᵉᶜʰⁿᶦᵠᵘᵉ ᵖʳᵒᵍʳᵃᵐ, ᵗʰᵉ ᵉˢˢᵉⁿᵗᶦᵃˡ ᶠᶦʳˢᵗ ˢᵗᵉᵖ ᶠᵒʳ ᵃˡˡ ˢᵗᵘᵈᵉⁿᵗˢ ᵃᵗ ᵗʰᵉ ˢᵗᵘᵈᶦᵒ.🌟 ᴵⁿ ᵗʰᶦˢ ᶜᵒᵐᵖʳᵉʰᵉⁿˢᶦᵛᵉ ᶜᵒᵘʳˢᵉ, ʸᵒᵘ’ˡˡ ᵉˣᵖˡᵒʳᵉ: 🔹• ᴴᵒʷᵃʳᵈ ᶠᶦⁿᵉ’ˢ ˢᶜʳᶦᵖᵗ ᴬⁿᵃˡʸˢᶦˢ ᴸᵉᶜᵗᵘʳᵉ 🔹• ᵁᵗᵃ ᴴᵃᵍᵉⁿ’ˢ ᴼᵇʲᵉᶜᵗ ᴱˣᵉʳᶜᶦˢᵉˢ 🔹• ᴾᵉʳˢᵒⁿᵃˡᶦᶻᵃᵗᶦᵒⁿ, ˢᵉⁿˢᵒʳʸ ʷᵒʳᵏ, ᵃⁿᵈ ᵉᵐᵒᵗᶦᵒⁿᵃˡ ʳᵉᶜᵃˡˡ ʷᶦᵗʰ ᴰᵃᵛᶦᵈ ᶜᵒᵘʳʸ 🔹• ᴴᵒʷ ᵗᵒ ʰᵃⁿᵈˡᵉ ⁿᵉʳᵛᵉˢ ᵃⁿᵈ ᵇʳᵉᵃᵏ ᵒˡᵈ ʰᵃᵇᶦᵗˢ 🔹• ᴬˢˢᶦᵍⁿᵉᵈ ᵐᵃᵗᵉʳᶦᵃˡ ᵃⁿᵈ ᵃⁿ ᶦⁿᵗʳᵒ ᵗᵒ ˢᶜᵉⁿᵉ ˢᵗᵘᵈʸ ᵀʰᶦˢ ᶜᵒᵘʳˢᵉ ᵍᵒᵉˢ ᵇᵉʸᵒⁿᵈ ᵃᵘᵈᶦᵗᶦᵒⁿ ᵖʳᵉᵖ, ᶠᵒᶜᵘˢᶦⁿᵍ ᵒⁿ ᵗʰᵉ ᵉⁿᵗᶦʳᵉ ᶜʳᵃᶠᵗ ᵒᶠ ᵃᶜᵗᶦⁿᵍ ᵗᵒ ʰᵉˡᵖ ʸᵒᵘ ⁿᵒᵗ ᵒⁿˡʸ ᵇᵒᵒᵏ ᵗʰᵉ ʲᵒᵇ ᵇᵘᵗ ⁿᵃᶦˡ ᵗʰᵉ ʳᵒˡᵉ ᵒⁿ ˢᵉᵗ ᵒʳ ˢᵗᵃᵍᵉ.🎥 📚ᴿᵉᵠᵘᶦʳᵉᵈ ᵀᵉˣᵗˢ: 🔹• ᴬ ᶜʰᵃˡˡᵉⁿᵍᵉ ᶠᵒʳ ᵗʰᵉ ᴬᶜᵗᵒʳ ᵇʸ ᵁᵗᵃ ᴴᵃᵍᵉⁿ 🔹• ᶠᶦⁿᵉ ᴼⁿ ᴬᶜᵗᶦⁿᵍ: ᴬ ⱽᶦˢᶦᵒⁿ ᵒᶠ ᵗʰᵉ ᶜʳᵃᶠᵗ ᵇʸ ᴴᵒʷᵃʳᵈ ᶠᶦⁿᵉ ᴮᵉ ˢᵘʳᵉ ᵗᵒ ᵍʳᵃᵇ ʸᵒᵘʳ ᵇᵒᵒᵏˢ ᵇᵉᶠᵒʳᵉ ᵗʰᵉ ᶠᶦʳˢᵗ ᶜˡᵃˢˢ ᵃⁿᵈ ˢᵗᵃʳᵗ ʳᵉᵃᵈᶦⁿᵍ ᴴᵒʷᵃʳᵈ’ˢ ᵇᵒᵒᵏ ᵗᵒ ᵈᶦᵛᵉ ᶦⁿᵗᵒ ᵗʰᶦˢ ᵗʳᵃⁿˢᶠᵒʳᵐᵃᵗᶦᵒⁿᵃˡ

An Asian kid was at an interview after winning a spelling bee, and the interviewer assumed he was a special child because of his achievement and intelligence. The kid’s calm demeanor and impressive performance only reinforced the interviewer’s assumptions, highlighting how accomplishments can sometimes lead people to make snap judgments about someone’s abilities or background.

A live AB test! Took the music off to see if it sorts the audio issues some people are having The story comes from Donald Rethke (aka Dr. Flush) the engineer who worked on the life support system designs for the Apollo missions. Michael Collins (Apollo 11) also mentions it in his book, but by his time the sizes were referred to as "extra large, immense, and unbelievable" - seems even astronauts need an ego boost from time to time!

yun_the_Pineapple_ sent an attachment.
I thought they were joking. I mean, how could a name stretch that far, like a rope being pulled from the ocean floor, syllable after syllable refusing to end? But then it hit me—the fish was real, and so was its name. Humu-humu-nuku-nuku-a-puaʻa. By the time you finish saying it, the tide has already gone out and come back in. A name so long you could lose your breath halfway, so playful it sounds like it was written by waves crashing into a ukulele. And the fish itself? Small, colorful, patterned like an artist spilled paint across its body and decided not to clean it up. Sharp fins, pouty lips, eyes that don’t blink but somehow still look at you like they’re in on the joke. It doesn’t just swim—it struts, with a name so legendary it could make every goldfish jealous. Sometimes it’s not the size of the creature that matters, but the weight of the story it carries. The humu-humu-nuku-nuku-a-puaʻa is proof of that—tiny body, giant name, and an even bigger presence. It reminds us that some things in life are meant to be said slowly, savored like music, because sometimes the beauty isn’t only in what you see, but in what you say.

𝐈𝐧 𝐭𝐨𝐝𝐚𝐲'𝐬 𝐞𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐨𝐟 #𝟏𝟎𝐓𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬, 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐧𝐝 𝐚 𝐜𝐨𝐳𝐲 𝐟𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐝𝐚𝐲 𝐚𝐭 𝐡𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐢𝐧 𝐋𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬 @𝐲𝐚𝐳𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐝 (𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐲) 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐬𝐜𝐫𝐢𝐩𝐭𝐬, 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐩𝐚𝐫𝐤 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐝𝐨𝐠 𝐊𝐨𝐤𝐨 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐦𝐚𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚 𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐦 𝐜𝐮𝐩 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐞𝐚 (𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐟𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐬𝐜𝐨𝐨𝐩𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐬𝐮𝐠𝐚𝐫, 𝐧𝐨 𝐥𝐞𝐬𝐬)🐾💗 "𝐌𝐲 𝐟𝐚𝐯𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐭 𝐋𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐨𝐮𝐥𝐝 𝐛𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐜𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐈 𝐠𝐞𝐭 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐰𝐚𝐥𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐬𝐞𝐞𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐲𝐨𝐧𝐞'𝐬 𝐢𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐯𝐢𝐝𝐮𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲," 𝐬𝐚𝐲𝐬 𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧. "𝐈 𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐟𝐞𝐞𝐥 𝐥𝐢𝐤𝐞 𝐈 𝐟𝐢𝐭 𝐢𝐧 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐟𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐥𝐲." 𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧, 𝐰𝐡𝐨 𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐲𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐫𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐞𝐫 𝐄𝐥𝐥𝐞 𝐢𝐧 "𝐇𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐬𝐭𝐨𝐩𝐩𝐞𝐫" 𝐨𝐧 @𝐧𝐞𝐭𝐟𝐥𝐢𝐱𝐮𝐤 𝐚𝐥𝐬𝐨 𝐫𝐞𝐟𝐥𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐨𝐧 𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐞𝐱𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐟𝐢𝐥𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐒𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐨𝐧 𝟑, 𝐰𝐡𝐢𝐜𝐡 𝐫𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐞𝐝 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐰𝐞𝐞𝐤. "𝐁𝐞𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞 𝐭𝐨 𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐲 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐭𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐄𝐥𝐥𝐞 𝐡𝐚𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐬𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐚𝐬 𝐬𝐨 𝐠𝐨𝐨𝐝 𝐛𝐞𝐜𝐚𝐮𝐬𝐞 𝐈'𝐦 𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐲𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐦𝐲𝐬𝐞𝐥𝐟, 𝐛𝐚𝐬𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐲. 𝐈'𝐦 𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐝 𝐨𝐟 𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐄𝐥𝐥𝐞 𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐲 𝐬𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐨𝐧.

yun_the_Pineapple_ sent an attachment.
Finnish Championships warm up for Dressage Champion class🔥🏆🇫🇮 My start delayed 25min and let me tell you… that was hard and painful. Not only keeping up the warm, energy and adrenaline pumping whole that time. Also back in my head was the truth about I should be already in the show jumping arena for judging. Competition day can always change and no can do. People try their best but it’s hard. Specially if you start first and you have to trust the starting time and target the warmup with the right time. 25 min is long time to wait. But we made it and I had good adrenaline in the arena!🙏🏻 #hobbyhorsing #hobbyhorses #hobbyhorsereels #hobbyhorse #keppihevoset #hobbyhorsecompetition #hobbyhorsedressage #dressage #keppihevostensm #keppihevostensm2025 #suomenmestaruus #adrenaline #warmup Cover: lotta

𝐈𝐧 𝐭𝐨𝐝𝐚𝐲’𝐬 𝐞𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐨𝐟 #𝟏𝟎𝐓𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬, 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐧𝐝 𝐚 𝐜𝐨𝐳𝐲 𝐟𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐝𝐚𝐲 𝐚𝐭 𝐡𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐢𝐧 𝐋𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬 @𝐲𝐚𝐳𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐝 (𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐲) 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐬𝐜𝐫𝐢𝐩𝐭𝐬, 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐩𝐚𝐫𝐤 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐝𝐨𝐠 𝐊𝐨𝐤𝐨 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐦𝐚𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚 𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐦 𝐜𝐮𝐩 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐞𝐚 (𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐟𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐬𝐜𝐨𝐨𝐩𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐬𝐮𝐠𝐚𝐫, 𝐧𝐨 𝐥𝐞𝐬𝐬)🐾💗 “𝐌𝐲 𝐟𝐚𝐯𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐭 𝐋𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐨𝐮𝐥𝐝 𝐛𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐜𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐈 𝐠𝐞𝐭 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐰𝐚𝐥𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐬𝐞𝐞𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐲𝐨𝐧𝐞’𝐬 𝐢𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐯𝐢𝐝𝐮𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲,” 𝐬𝐚𝐲𝐬 𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧. “𝐈 𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐟𝐞𝐞𝐥 𝐥𝐢𝐤𝐞 𝐈 𝐟𝐢𝐭 𝐢𝐧 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐟𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐥𝐲.” 𝐘𝐚𝐬𝐦𝐢𝐧, 𝐰𝐡𝐨 𝐩𝐥𝐚𝐲𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐫𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐞𝐫 𝐄𝐥𝐥𝐞 𝐢𝐧 “𝐇𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐬𝐭𝐨𝐩𝐩𝐞𝐫” 𝐨𝐧 @𝐧𝐞𝐭𝐟𝐥𝐢𝐱𝐮𝐤 𝐚𝐥𝐬𝐨 𝐫𝐞𝐟𝐥𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐨𝐧 𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐞𝐱𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐟𝐢𝐥𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐒𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐨𝐧 𝟑, 𝐰𝐡𝐢𝐜𝐡 𝐫𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐞𝐝

EDIT: Please look at my page before you comment something mean and see that I am a comedian and we are both nonbinary and bisexual. This was a silly joke and commentary on bi erasure and the judgement we have gotten from both queer and straight people. The point of the first statement *in quotation marks* was to signify the fact that *other people* make these comments and assume we are gay and lesbian. We can't be sure why people assume but it may be because we are nonbinary and also just our general aura. Hope we can move past this as a community and realize that gender and sexuality is fluid and try and assume good intentions from random queer people on the internet tysm ❤️ — Turns out we just are good old fashioned bisexuals also wedding in less than 30 days! #engaged #queer #wedding #queerproposal #standup #bisexual

You sent an attachment.
Why should a man who spent years in the shadows, grinding in silence, sacrificing his time, health, relationships, and sometimes even his sanity, be expected to hand over half of his life’s work simply because a relationship ended? A man who built himself from nothing-brick by brick, failure by failure -knows what every dollar means. It wasn’t gifted to him. He earned it in the dark while nobody was watching. He fought battles with himself every day, pushed through loneliness, ignored temptations, and kept his vision alive when no one else believed. He wasn’t handed support, he created his own structure, often while being doubted, laughed at, or completely ignored. Divorce settlements have become less about fairness and more about entitlement. They reward departure instead of resilience, and in many cases, punish success rather than protect equity. Love should never be tied to the fear of losing what you bled for. If we truly respected ambition, sacrifice, and personal growth, then the laws would reflect that. No man-or woman-should be forced to surrender their life’s mission just because someone decided they no longer wanted to be part of it. Respect the hustle. Respect the legacy. And most importantly, respect the individual who never quit on himself, even when the world did. Speaker: Donald J Trump
mindsetoftruth

You sent an attachment.
Cooking Up A Storm! . Excited to join Chef Nick once again to explore one of my favorite sound effects — the sound of rain! It’s quite difficult to record the sound of rain in a precise way, because 1) it needs to be raining and 2) rain is wet! So almost any time you hear rain in a cartoon or movie it’s usually the sound of something frying in a pan. Isn’t that cool? . If you concentrate just on the animated clip, you will experience an auditory illusion where your brain will slowly shift from hearing the sound as a sizzling steak to hearing it as rain. How cool! This is some farm-fresh foley! Bon appetit :) . #rhythmsofcomedy #food #steak #anime #rain #soundeffects #satisfying #ghibli #behindthescenes #moviemagic #musician #chef
josh.harmon

You sent an attachment.
No problem! Here’s the information about the Pound-Rebka Experiment and Gravitational Redshift 🌌📡 🔹 What Was the Pound-Rebka Experiment? • Conducted in 1959 by Robert Pound and Glen Rebka, this experiment provided direct evidence of gravitational redshift, a prediction of Einstein’s general relativity. 🌍📊 🔹 Key Setup • A gamma-ray source was placed at the top of a 22.5-meter tower at Harvard University, while a detector was placed at the bottom. 📡⬇️ • As gamma rays traveled downward, their frequency was slightly increased due to the stronger gravitational field near the base. This effect is explained by the energy loss or gain of light in a gravitational field. ⚡ 🔹 How Was it Measured? • The team used the Mössbauer effect, which measures tiny shifts in frequency with extreme precision, to detect the change in gamma-ray frequency. 🔬📐 🔹 Result? • They confirmed the predicted redshift within 1% accuracy, validating Einstein’s theory that light changes frequency in gravitational fields. 🌌✅ 🔹 Impact on Physics • This was a major breakthrough, demonstrating that time runs slower in stronger gravitational fields and proving general relativity’s accuracy in everyday physics. 🕒🌠 ✨ QUIZ TIME ✨ What did the Pound-Rebka experiment confirm? a) Gravity doesn’t affect light ⚪ b) Light loses energy in gravitational fields 🟠 c) Gravity only affects massive objects ⚫

You sent an attachment.
Skills and Drills: Ep 1 🧠 Introducing a new series for you guys where I’ll be showing you some fun, impactful drills and skill progressions to try out yourself or with you team. Episode 1 is showing off a fantastic scrum primer I like to use to build the anti rotational strength necessary for forwards to dominate in the scrum. I like to put this in as a primer before our main scrum session after a general warmup. I usually start with 20 seconds of work and 40 seconds rest and progress towards a 1:1 work rest ratio over 3/4 weeks then I’ll change it up. Get partners working together on a running clock, partner 1 goes at the top of the minute and partner 2 goes on the 30 seconds. 3 sets should do the trick. Send this to someone who needs a stronger core! Keep Stacking Days 🧱 WalkTheWalk

DO YOU AGREE? 🇧🇬🎤 Follow @Bulgaria 🎥 Unknown | DM for credit Bulgaria covers about 111,000 square kilometers in Southeast Europe. Our land stretches from the Danube River in the north to the Black Sea in the east, down to the Rhodope Mountains near Greece. Mountains take up a third of the country, plains another third, and rivers, valleys, and coasts make the rest. It is a crossroads of Europe and Asia, guarded by the Balkans, fed by the Danube, and opened to the sea.

yun_the_Pineapple_

yun_the_Pineapple_ sent an attachment.
#謬思時尚 Sunnei 是於 2014 年在米蘭創立的義大利新銳時裝品牌，由 Simone Rizzo 與 Loris Messina 共同創辦。兩人並非傳統服裝科班出身，一位是時尚採購出身，一位是視覺設計師，他們觀察到義大利缺乏真正前衛且兼具實穿性的年輕品牌，遂決定大膽重新定義米蘭時尚。 Sunnei 的設計以日常可穿卻充滿實驗感聞名，融合乾淨線條、幾何比例與活潑色彩，同時打破男女裝界線，展現無性別與包容。品牌的溝通語言輕鬆、不拘泥傳統，常以幽默或非典型的秀場形式引發話題，例如舉辦互動性高的服裝發表會，並邀請觀眾參與產品定製。 近年 Sunnei 積極探索新零售與品牌體驗，在米蘭總部開設「沒有概念的概念店」，結合商店、咖啡館、展覽與社群活動，打破零售空間的單一功能，讓消費者能直接接觸品牌創作過程並與設計師互動。品牌也推出Canvas 計畫，讓顧客直接進入採購流程，在平台上選擇布料，創造個性化單品，展現顛覆傳統供應鏈的企圖心。 Sunnei 雖贏得不少評論家與時尚編輯欣賞，但也有媒體將他們的風格形容為「adult kindergarten aesthetic（成人的幼稚審美）」，這樣的評價褒貶各半，認同其原創性卻也質疑成熟度與設計深度。
nonsense.lev
https://www.instagram.com/reel/DPGd8Smic8b/?id=3730801027814379291_56133949564

~Green Turtle Stack~ 🥇This is the photo that got me gold at the “UNDERWATER AWARDS AUSTRALASIA 2025” imaging competition for the Smartphone/Action Cam category 💪🏽😬  Ningaloo Reef is a place of infinite possibilities, where anything and everything might appear. On this day, I was crossing the vast sand flats after visiting the local manta cleaning station, and was stunned when a big, odd looking figure emerged ahead; it was the sight of three turtles stacked atop one another. An unforgettable moment. ✨How the story ended, I cannot say; but I like to think that next season, green turtle hatchlings will carry the sequel forward. #oceanart #underwaterphotography #ausgeo #seaturtle #underwatercompetition #underwaterawards #madewithlightroom
luchi.in.the.jungle

Follow (us) @ExplainingACar to learn something NEW about vehicles every day 🚗💫 Zero-tolerance machining is ultra-precise manufacturing where parts are made with extremely tight tolerances — meaning there’s virtually no room for error in dimensions. This ensures components fit together perfectly, reducing friction, wear, and failure. It’s commonly used in aerospace, medical devices, and high-performance engines, where even the thickness of a human hair could throw off performance. Let us know your thoughts below! • #engineering #explore #mechanics #cars #learn
